creator: natbsd@instinctive.eu
text:
Hello,

on Monday 24 February 2014 at 13:56, René Ladan wrote:
> Since graphics/luxrender 0.7.1 has been on the remove list for way too long:
> 
> - Natacha, would you mind taking over this port (given that Alexey is OK) ?

   Sure, assuming we manage to get something working. However I have
had no confirmation (or any communication) from Alexey.

> - I tried to update this port to 1.2.1 but the distribution file is no
> longer available,

   I still have the distfiles for both luxrays and luxrender 1.2.1. I
can upload them somewhere. Is there a sort-of official place for that,
or should I just put them on my personal webserver (considering the
popularity of the port it should be too much of a load)?

   Is there a policy or something regarding keeping old versions
available, should 1.3.1 prove unportable?

>                   I have a work-in-progress patch to update it to 1.3.1
> instead available at
> 
> http://people.freebsd.org/~rene/patches/luxrender-131.diff
> 
> which also requires luxrays to update to 1.3.1, available here:
> http://people.freebsd.org/~rene/patches/luxrays-131.diff
> 
> The latter now needs to be built with smallluxgpu4 , otherwise luxrender
> 1.3.1 refuses to build. Unfortunately, smallluxgpu in luxrays 1.3.1 does
> not support FreeBSD yet.

   SmallLuxGPU is a test suite for GPGPU, so unless we get a working
implementation of OpenCL on FreeBSD, there is no hope to get it working.

   I will take a closer look when I have some time (probably next
week-end), whether it can be made to work on pure CPU or worked around
or something.



Thanks for making this move,
Natcha Porté
